2606.25632.title: Staying In Character: Perspective-Bounded Memory For Book-Based Role-Playing Agents
2606.25632.paper_summary: Recent LLM role-playing systems build character agents from novels by extracting characters, scenes, and relations. Yet long-narrative role-playing suffers from two failures: Factual Overreach, where shared retrieval or parametric memory lets a character use facts outside its perspective, and Stylistic Monotony, where profile descriptions flatten a character into a fixed voice. To address these failures, we propose REVERIEMEM, a three-layer memory architecture for book-based character agents. The episodic layer stores first-person scene memories; the semantic layer stores visibility-tagged facts; and the personality layer stores situation-dependent speech and behaviour patterns. For evaluation, we construct KBF-QA, a 4,386-question benchmark over eight novels for testing knowledge boundaries. REVERIEMEM improves Knowledge Boundary Fidelity by 34.6 percentage points over the strongest prior method. On BOOKWORLD's five-dimension pairwise narrative protocol, REVERIEMEM achieves a ~ 79% win rate, suggesting that perspective-bounded memory improves both boundary fidelity and character-grounded narrative generation.
2606.25632.paper_summary_zh: 最近的LLM角色扮演系統通過提取角色、場景和關係，從小說中構建角色代理。<lb/>然而，長篇敘事角色扮演面臨兩個失敗：事實過度擴展，當共享檢索或參數記憶讓角色使用超出其視角的事實時，以及風格單調，當檔案描述將角色壓縮成固定的聲音時。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了REVERIEMEM，一種基於書籍的角色代理的三層記憶架構。<lb/>情節層存儲第一人稱場景記憶；語義層存儲可見性標記的事實；而個性層存儲情境依賴的語言和行為模式。<lb/>為了進行評估，我們構建了KBF-QA，一個涵蓋八部小說的4,386個問題基準，用於測試知識邊界。<lb/>REVERIEMEM在知識邊界保真度上比最強的先前方法提高了34.6個百分點。<lb/>在BOOKWORLD的五維成對敘事協議中，REVERIEMEM達到了約79%的勝率，這表明受視角限制的記憶改善了邊界保真度和角色基礎的敘事生成。
2606.25632.author: Xushuo Tang et.al.
2606.25632.authors: Xushuo Tang, Junhe Zhang, Zihan Yang, Yifu Tang, Sichao Li, Longbin Lai, Zhengyi Yang
